title: Rubus philyrophyllus
taxonomy: Rubus philyrophyllus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). It belongs to the genus Rubus, which includes blackberries, raspberries, and thimbleberries. As a member of the genus Rubus, it is further categorized as a bramble, a type of shrub or vine with thorns or prickles. The classification of Rubus philyrophyllus is still being researched and may be subject to change as new information becomes available. In general, its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the rose family.

intro: Rubus phlyrophyllus, also known as the Japanese bramble or Japanese raspberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever reduction, pain relief, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Rubus phlyrophyllus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Rubus phlyrophyllus is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments.
